title: Implement update_category tool: rename, change notes, move between groups
description:
Add a write MCP tool wrapping CategoriesApi.update_category().

## Background
Audit mcp-ynab-zt1 found this is the highest-value missing write tool. Originally triggered by user trying to create a 'Financial Planning (Future)' category — creation is impossible via YNAB API, but rename/move IS possible.

## Implementation

SDK call: CategoriesApi.update_category(budget_id, category_id, PatchCategoryWrapper(category=SaveCategory(...)))

SaveCategory writable fields:
- name: str — rename the category
- note: str — update the note/memo  
- category_group_id: str — move to a different category group

Response: SaveCategoryResponse with updated Category object.

## Where to add
src/mcp_ynab/tools/budgeting.py — near get_categories/get_category_for_month

## Tool design
- Tool name: update_category
- Params: category_id, name=None, note=None, category_group_id=None (at least one required)
- Annotation: IDEMPOTENT_MUTATING_TOOL
- Look up category_id by name via existing category cache if needed

## Tests
Follow pattern in tests/test_server.py — mock CategoriesApi constructor, test rename/note/move paths.
close_reason: Implemented update_category tool wrapping CategoriesApi.update_category(). Supports rename, note update, and move-to-group. 4 tests added to test_budgeting_core.py. All 247 tests pass.

title: Tool: move_money — reallocate between two categories
description:
Implements YNAB Rule 3 ('roll with the punches').

Convenience tool: takes amount, from_category_id, to_category_id, month, budget_id. Internally:
1. GETs current budgeted for both categories that month
2. PATCHes from_category to (current - amount)
3. PATCHes to_category to (current + amount)

Annotation: MUTATING (not idempotent — running twice doubles the move).

Caveat: not transactional in YNAB. If step 3 fails, document that the user must manually reconcile or rerun. Consider an explicit error message that includes the partially-applied state.
notes:

- New top-level @mcp.tool/resource/prompt; touches no existing functions.
- After mcp-ynab-0bw lands, this task targets a distinct module — fully parallel.

- Can run alongside ANY other 'parallel-safe' task in the same wave label (wave-1, wave-2, wave-3).

title: Budgeting Core: assign/move money + monthly view
description:
The server is a transaction logger; it cannot do the YNAB Method's core verbs.

Missing capabilities:
- Rule 1 (give every dollar a job): assign $ to a category for a month
- Rule 3 (roll with the punches): move $ from category A → B
- Rule 4 (age your money): read Age of Money / Ready to Assign
- Monthly snapshot: budgeted vs activity vs balance per category

This epic covers the highest-leverage gap: making the server usable for the actual work people open YNAB to do. Children include get_month/get_category_for_month tools, assign_money/move_money mutations, and month-snapshot MCP resources.

YNAB API endpoints involved:
- GET /budgets/{id}/months
- GET /budgets/{id}/months/{month}
- GET /budgets/{id}/months/{month}/categories/{cat_id}
- PATCH /budgets/{id}/months/{month}/categories/{cat_id}
notes:
PARALLEL DISPATCH PLAYBOOK
=========================
This corpus is labeled for an orchestrator LLM to fan out parallel sub-agents.

Labels:
  parallel-safe       — pure-add (new function only). Fan out freely.
  parallel-serialize  — modifies an existing function. Check zone:* label.
  parallel-gating     — blocks deeper parallelism (file split, helper factor).
  zone:<symbol>       — names a function/class. Tasks sharing a zone MUST serialize.
  wave-N              — recommended dispatch wave.

Run order:
  1. Wave 0  (gating):       mcp-ynab-0bw (server.py split) + g9z.7 (api helper)
                              → Land first to unlock true cross-file parallelism.
  2. Wave 1  (~17 agents):   all parallel-safe tools/resources from epics jwa/htf/3l3/g9z.
  3. Wave 1b (~5 agents):    parallel-serialize bug/typing fixes (each in its own zone).
  4. Wave 2  (~4 agents):    resources & elicitations whose dependencies cleared.
  5. Wave 2-serial-create_txn: qlh.1 → qlh.2 → qlh.3 (chain modifies create_transaction).
  6. Wave 3  (~6 agents):    @mcp.prompt definitions, all parallel-safe.

Conflict avoidance: the orchestrator should query 'bd show <id>' for each candidate,
read the zone:* labels, and refuse to dispatch two agents whose zone labels intersect.

Per-task hint: tasks include their own 'PARALLEL' note describing what they can /
cannot run alongside. The orchestrator should read those notes before fanning out.
close_reason: Implemented in commit on main: month snapshot + assign_money/move_money tools and 2 month resources, with shared _resolve_month helper, MonthsApi/PatchMonthCategoryWrapper/SaveMonthCategory re-exports, 10 new unit tests, and 1 live integration smoke. 52 unit tests pass; lint and 100% docstring coverage clean.

title: Split server.py 735-line monolith into focused modules
description: server.py currently holds the FastMCP instance, all 9 tools, 2 resources, AsyncYNABClient, _get_client factory, YNABResources persistence class, markdown formatters, and helpers. Split into: client.py (AsyncYNABClient + _get_client + YNABResources), formatters.py (markdown table builders), tools.py (tool functions), resources.py (resource handlers). Keep mcp = FastMCP('YNAB') in server.py as the import surface so existing entry point still works. This is a pure refactor — no behavior change. Land as its own commit so the diff is reviewable.
acceptance_criteria: server.py reduced to FastMCP instance + imports. New modules have focused responsibilities. All existing tests pass with no changes. mcp-ynab CLI still launches.
notes:
Deferred from session 2026-05-03. The split is feasible but riskier than its value because tests/test_tools.py patches server.BudgetsApi/AccountsApi/etc and references server.get_budgets, server.ynab_resources, server.YNABResources directly. Safe approach for next attempt: keep mcp = FastMCP, ynab_resources, and YNABResources in server.py; move helpers and tool functions into formatters.py / tools.py / client.py; import the new modules at the BOTTOM of server.py so @mcp.tool registration runs after mcp is bound, AND re-export the moved tool callables so server.get_budgets etc still resolve. Land separately.
PARALLELISM MULTIPLIER — promoted to P1.

This is no longer a code-cleanliness task. With server.py split into focused modules (mcp_ynab/tools/budgeting.py, transactions.py, payees.py, analysis.py; mcp_ynab/resources.py; mcp_ynab/prompts.py; mcp_ynab/elicit.py), the ~17 pure-add tool/resource tasks in the new corpus become FULLY parallelizable across distinct files instead of merge-conflict-serializable on a single file.

Recommended split target:
  src/mcp_ynab/
    server.py            # FastMCP instance, AsyncYNABClient, registration glue (re-export tools/resources/prompts for test patch compatibility)
    api_helpers.py       # _get_client, _resolve_config_dir, APIs(client) factory (g9z.7)
    formatters.py        # _build_markdown_table, _format_accounts_output, _format_dollar_amount
    state.py             # YNABResources
    tools/
      budgeting.py       # get_month, assign_money, move_money, get_category_for_month
      transactions.py    # create/categorize/approve/update/delete/bulk/split/import
      analysis.py        # spending_by_*, get_transactions_by_category
      payees.py          # get_payees, rename_payee, scheduled
      diagnostics.py     # ping/whoami
    resources.py         # all @mcp.resource decorators
    prompts.py           # all @mcp.prompt decorators
    elicit.py            # shared elicitation helpers (_resolve_budget_id, etc.)

Re-export pattern for backward-compat with existing tests that patch server.BudgetsApi etc.: import * from submodules at the bottom of server.py.

DEPENDENTS: most P1/P2 tasks in epics jwa, htf, 3l3 become more dispatchable after this. Consider running this in a dedicated worktree first, then fanning out parallel agents on the new modules.

All 42 unit tests pass; 9 integration tests collect cleanly; CLI launches; 10 tools + 2 resources register; 100% docstring coverage maintained. title: Runner hardening: subprocess isolation + OS resource limits
description:
## Problem

The in-process runner in src/mcp_ynab/code_mode/runner.py is documented as a 'convenience layer, not a Python security boundary' (runner.py:3-5). Known gaps:

1. asyncio.wait_for cannot interrupt synchronous blocking user code (tracked separately as mcp-ynab-fkv with PoC from coderabbitai PR #7 review).
2. AST audit is best-effort — known Python sandbox bypasses (__class__.__base__.__subclasses__(), f-string interpolation, async generator .athrow, dunder lookups via getattr with non-literal arg) are not all closed.
3. No OS-level resource limits (CPU, memory, wall clock from outside the interpreter).
4. No network isolation — code can in principle reach the network through any tool that does outbound HTTP (currently mediated by ynab.read/ynab.write, but a future bug could leak this).

## Required work

Move user-code execution out of the parent process. Two viable shapes:

(a) multiprocessing.Process with hard kill on timeout + pickled-result IPC; ynab proxy bridges via Pipe/Queue using a small RPC protocol.
(b) Subprocess-per-call via subprocess.Popen running a small worker script that imports the MCP registry fresh; proxy calls go back over stdio JSON-RPC.

Either way, must preserve:
- CodeModeResult schema (ok, result, logs, error, traceback, truncated)
- stdout capture (logs field)
- Pydantic model results flow through (to_jsonable_python at the boundary)
- ynab.read / ynab.write proxy split + mutations_enabled gating
- AST audit still runs in-parent before spawning the child

## Constraints

- The proxy can't be pickled — closures over YNABResources, httpx.AsyncClient, FastMCP registry. Need either an RPC bridge or rebuild-in-child + IPC.
- resource.setrlimit (RLIMIT_CPU, RLIMIT_AS) for memory/CPU caps where supported.
- Consider seccomp on Linux to drop syscalls the snippet can't possibly need.
- Document threat model update in docs/code-mode-plan.md once landed.

## Related

- mcp-ynab-fkv (asyncio timeout limitation — this task absorbs the fix)
- docs/code-mode-plan.md §0 'Still open' first bullet, §4.1 Phase 3 row, §6 cmd.6 row

title: [epic] Preferences system: keychain, typed config, cache TTL
description:
Generalize the existing YNABResources file-backed store into a real preferences subsystem.

Decisions (from user, 2026-05-05):
1. Add OS keychain (keyring lib) as an optional storage path for YNAB_API_KEY. Env var still works; keychain takes precedence when set.
2. Flat Preferences shape (single Pydantic model). No nested groups for now.
3. Category cache uses TTL-based expiry, default 7 days (10080 min), with an explicit force-refresh path.

Scope sketch (subtasks to break out):
- Add 'keyring' dependency, write _resolve_api_key() with env > keychain > error.
- Introduce Preferences pydantic model + pydantic-settings layering (env > preferences.json > defaults).
- Migrate YNABResources to read/write preferences.json; keep get_preferred_budget_id / set_preferred_budget_id as compatibility shims.
- Add 'last_refreshed' timestamp to category cache, TTL check in _find_category_id and resource lookups.
- Add MCP tools: get_preferences, set_preference, refresh_categories(force=True), set_api_key(keychain=True).
- Migration: lift values from existing preferred_budget_id.json / budget_category_cache.json into preferences.json on first load.
notes:
Design refinements after advisor review (2026-05-05):
- Skip pydantic-settings; use plain Pydantic BaseModel for 3 fields. Revisit only if scope grows.
- One-shot migration on first load: lift legacy files into preferences.json then delete them. No compat shims (avoids two sources of truth).
- API key precedence: env > keychain > error. Env wins so CI / shell overrides remain easy. Keychain is durable storage.
- Keyring backend errors (headless Linux, Docker without secret-service) must be caught in _resolve_api_key and fall through with a debug log — not propagate.
- Open question for 6ha.4: when confirm_before_post becomes a preference, does the per-call param override or does the pref disable the param? Defer until 6ha.4 and ask user.

title: Feature: fuzzy + cached _find_category_id
description:
Today _find_category_id (server.py ~290) is exact case-insensitive against a fresh CategoriesApi.get_categories call. Two problems:
1. 'groceries' silently misses 'Groceries 🛒' or 'Groceries (& Household)'
2. Refetches every call instead of using YNABResources cache

Fix:
- Read from cache first; refresh on miss
- Use difflib.get_close_matches (cutoff=0.6) for fuzzy
- Return either a single id or a list of candidates so the caller can elicit choice (Epic 4)
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-serialize, zone:_find_category_id + zone:YNABResources.
- Refactors _find_category_id and adds cache-read in YNABResources.
- Serialize after g9z.2 (also zone:YNABResources).
- BLOCKS qlh.2 (elicit category) — qlh.2 wants the multi-candidate return shape from this work.
close_reason: Implemented fuzzy + cached _find_category_id with three-tier matching (exact → substring → difflib) and bounded stale-cache refresh; 8 new unit tests; unblocks qlh.2

title: Elicit: budget when no preference set
description:
Today create_transaction & get_account_balance silently grab budgets[0] when YNABResources.preferred_budget_id is None. Silent foot-gun for users with multiple budgets.

Replace the fallback with ctx.elicit(...) presenting the budget list. On selection, optionally also call set_preferred_budget_id.

Touches: create_transaction (server.py ~310), get_account_balance (~339), and any future tool that defaults to budgets[0]. Factor a single helper: async def _resolve_budget_id(ctx) -> str.
notes:
PARALLEL: parallel-serialize, zone:create_transaction + zone:get_account_balance.
- Replaces silent budgets[0] fallback in create_transaction AND get_account_balance with elicit-then-resolve helper.
- SERIAL CHAIN with qlh.2, qlh.3 on create_transaction. Run order: qlh.1 → qlh.2 → qlh.3.
- Conflicts with g9z.1 (overlaps on get_account_balance). Recommend: fold g9z.1 fix into this task, then drop g9z.1.
- Safe alongside ANY task that doesn't touch create_transaction or get_account_balance.
close_reason: Replaced silent budgets[0] fallback in create_transaction & get_account_balance with shared _resolve_budget_id helper that elicits via ctx.elicit when multiple budgets exist; raises on ctx=None+multi-budget so the foot-gun is gone.

title: Prompt: monthly_budget_check
description:
@mcp.prompt for end-of-month / mid-month sanity check:
1. get_month for current month
2. Identify categories where activity > budgeted (overspending)
3. Suggest move_money from underspent categories to cover
4. Surface Age of Money trend if available

title: Elicitations for safer interactive tool flows
description:
The server has silent-failure modes that erode trust:
- create_transaction & get_account_balance silently grab budgets[0] when no preferred budget is set
- create_transaction silently accepts category_name='' or a misspelled name and posts uncategorized
- mutations never confirm before committing

MCP elicitation lets a tool pause mid-execution and ask the user for structured input. Use it to:
- elicit a budget when none preferred (replace silent budgets[0] fallback)
- elicit a category from the cached list when create_transaction's category_name is missing or fuzzy-matches multiple
- confirm before posting create_transaction
- confirm before delete_transaction (Epic 2)
- elicit source/destination categories in move_money (Epic 1)

mcp[cli]>=1.20 supports ctx.elicit() — no SDK upgrade needed.
notes:
PARALLEL DISPATCH PLAYBOOK
=========================
This corpus is labeled for an orchestrator LLM to fan out parallel sub-agents.

Labels:
  parallel-safe       — pure-add (new function only). Fan out freely.

  parallel-gating     — blocks deeper parallelism (file split, helper factor).
  zone:<symbol>       — names a function/class. Tasks sharing a zone MUST serialize.

title: Document YNAB API limitations: no category create, goals read-only
description:
Add documentation noting fundamental YNAB API v1 limitations discovered in audit mcp-ynab-zt1.

## Limitations to document
1. **Cannot create categories via API** — POST /categories doesn't exist in YNAB API v1. Categories can only be created in the YNAB UI.
2. **Goals are read-only** — The SaveCategory write model only exposes name/note/category_group_id. Goal fields (goal_type, goal_target, etc.) appear on the read Category model but CANNOT be set via API.
3. **Cannot update/delete scheduled transactions** — ScheduledTransactionsApi only has create and get methods; no update or delete endpoint.

## Where to document
- Consider a YNAB_API_NOTES.md or add to README/server docstring
- The code_mode.execute tool description could note these limitations
- Alternatively, add to the tool docstrings for related read tools (get_categories, get_scheduled_transactions)

title: code-mode: docs/code-mode-examples.md not included in installed wheel
description:
## Problem

_read_code_mode_examples() in src/mcp_ynab/resources.py loads docs/code-mode-examples.md via filesystem path search (repo root and CWD). The /docs directory is not included in hatchling packaging (pyproject.toml), so this raises FileNotFoundError when the package is run from an installed wheel.

## Location

src/mcp_ynab/resources.py — _read_code_mode_examples()

## Required fix

Ship the examples as package data and read via importlib.resources, or embed the content directly in the module. Example approach:

1. Move docs/code-mode-examples.md into src/mcp_ynab/ (or a subdirectory)
2. Add to pyproject.toml package-data includes
3. Read via importlib.resources.files('mcp_ynab').joinpath('code-mode-examples.md').read_text()

## Notes

Flagged by copilot-pull-request-reviewer on PR #7. Dev usage (running from repo root) is unaffected — only installed wheel deployments hit this.

title: Expand ynab://code-mode/examples with workflow snippets
description:
## Problem

docs/code-mode-examples.md (served via ynab://code-mode/examples) contains the initial curated examples landed in the MVP. As real usage uncovers good patterns, the examples corpus should grow.

## Candidate snippets to add (each ~10-15 lines)

- Bulk approve all unreviewed transactions from a specific account
- Categorize transactions by payee-name regex against a user-supplied map
- Reconcile: compare account balance vs YNAB-side sum for the last N days
- Monthly spending diff: this-month vs same-month-last-year, grouped by category
- Find and merge duplicate payees (Levenshtein ratio > threshold)
- Sweep available money: assign all unassigned dollars to a target category
- Detect category overspend and propose a move_money plan
- 'Triage' workflow: needs-attention transactions, grouped by likely cause

## Required work

1. Write each snippet as a complete async __main__ block that runs against the current ynab.read/ynab.write proxy.
2. Add a brief one-line description above each snippet.
3. Update the regression test pinning the examples-resource namespace style (currently test_code_mode_examples_resource_uses_current_namespaces in tests/test_server.py).
4. Keep total resource size under ~8KB.

## Related

- docs/code-mode-plan.md §0 'Still open' fourth bullet
- docs/code-mode-examples.md (existing examples for reference)

title: Stub generator quality: return types and model schemas
description:
## Problem

ynab://code-mode/stubs introspects the FastMCP tool registry and emits a .pyi-like surface, but the generated stubs are not yet a complete SDK reference. Known gaps:

- Many return types render as 'Any' because the tool functions return wrapped TextContent / list[TextContent] / dict at the FastMCP layer, masking the underlying YNAB SDK model.
- Pydantic model fields aren't exploded — the LLM has to guess transaction.payee_name vs transaction.payeeName.
- Parameter descriptions from Pydantic Field() metadata don't always make it into the stub docstring.
- No types are emitted for the YNAB SDK model classes that the proxied tools actually return.

## Required work

1. Walk each registered tool's source-level return annotation (not just the FastMCP-wrapped one) — use inspect.get_annotations(tool.fn) or unwrap any decorators.
2. For YNAB SDK model returns (TransactionDetail, CategoryGroupWithCategories, etc.), emit a Protocol or class stub listing field names + types.
3. Pull Field() description/title into the stub as inline comments next to each parameter.
4. Snapshot test: pin the generated .pyi against a golden file so changes are intentional.

## Constraints

- Stub size matters: the resource is delivered to every Code Mode user. Cap somewhere around ~5KB or paginate.
- Don't import the full ynab SDK at stub-gen time if it's slow — cache by tool name + signature hash.

## Related

- docs/code-mode-plan.md §0 'Still open' third bullet, §4.5 stub-generation sketch

## Note

Note: the stub/generator quality work now also feeds the search tool's spec/catalog object (build_spec in stubs.py). Catalog quality directly affects search usability — improving annotation coverage benefits both the .pyi resource and the search sandbox.

title: Replacement-mode visible-toolset policy
description: Pin the visible tool set to exactly {search, execute} with a regression test. Define the escape-hatch preference semantics: code_mode_replace_tools=False restores the full 34-tool surface. The questions from the original framing (is get_budgets the entry point? do set_api_key/etc. belong?) are resolved by the 2-tool pivot. Acceptance: a test asserts list_tools returns exactly {search, execute} when code_mode_replace_tools=True (default), and the full set when False.

title: code-mode: asyncio.wait_for cannot enforce timeout against synchronous blocking user code
description:
## Problem

asyncio.wait_for(main(), timeout=timeout_s) in run_code() cannot interrupt synchronous blocking code (time.sleep, CPU-bound loops) because the event loop never gets control back. The timeout is effectively unenforced for non-cooperative code.

PoC from coderabbitai PR #7 review: wait_for with timeout=0.1 still returns ~0.5s later when user code calls time.sleep(0.5).

## Location

src/mcp_ynab/code_mode/runner.py — run_code(), the await asyncio.wait_for(main(), timeout=timeout_s) call

## Required fix

Run user code in an isolated worker process (multiprocessing.Process or concurrent.futures.ProcessPoolExecutor) and hard-terminate it when timeout_s elapses. stdout/stderr must be piped back from the child.

## Constraints

- Must preserve stdout capture (redirect_stdout behavior)
- Must return CodeModeResult with the same schema
- The ynab proxy cannot be passed to a subprocess directly — need IPC or re-building the proxy in the child
- Adds significant serialization complexity for the result value

## Interim

Document the soft-timeout limitation clearly in code_mode/README.md until the full fix lands.
notes: Re-parented under mcp-ynab-fsv.1 (Runner hardening: subprocess isolation + OS resource limits). The subprocess rewrite that fsv.1 prescribes is a strict superset of the timeout fix this bug captures — fkv should close when fsv.1 lands. See docs/code-mode-plan.md §0 'Still open' first bullet.

title: Correctness, hygiene, and resource expansion
description:
Bugs and small improvements surfaced in the design review, plus expansion of MCP resources for cheap context.

Bugs:
- get_account_balance ignores set_preferred_budget_id and always picks budgets[0]
- ynab://categories/{budget_id} resource drops the 'group' field that's already cached

Quality / typing:
- Pydantic Literal for filter_type in get_transactions_needing_attention (replace hand-rolled string check)
- fuzzy + cached _find_category_id (today: exact case-insensitive — 'groceries' silently misses 'Groceries 🛒')
- document amount sign convention in create_transaction (negative=outflow, positive=inflow)

New surface:
- ping / whoami tool (auth smoke test)
- ynab://budgets resource
- ynab://accounts/{budget_id} resource
- enriched ynab://categories/{budget_id} including current-month budgeted/activity

Refactor:
- factor a single helper for constructing the API stubs (BudgetsApi/AccountsApi/...) to reduce per-tool boilerplate.
notes:
PARALLEL DISPATCH PLAYBOOK
=========================
This corpus is labeled for an orchestrator LLM to fan out parallel sub-agents.

Labels:
  parallel-safe       — pure-add (new function only). Fan out freely.
  parallel-serialize  — modifies an existing function. Check zone:* label.
  parallel-gating     — blocks deeper parallelism (file split, helper factor).
  zone:<symbol>       — names a function/class. Tasks sharing a zone MUST serialize.
  wave-N              — recommended dispatch wave.

Run order:
  1. Wave 0  (gating):       mcp-ynab-0bw (server.py split) + g9z.7 (api helper)
                              → Land first to unlock true cross-file parallelism.
  2. Wave 1  (~17 agents):   all parallel-safe tools/resources from epics jwa/htf/3l3/g9z.
  3. Wave 1b (~5 agents):    parallel-serialize bug/typing fixes (each in its own zone).
  4. Wave 2  (~4 agents):    resources & elicitations whose dependencies cleared.
  5. Wave 2-serial-create_txn: qlh.1 → qlh.2 → qlh.3 (chain modifies create_transaction).
  6. Wave 3  (~6 agents):    @mcp.prompt definitions, all parallel-safe.

Conflict avoidance: the orchestrator should query 'bd show <id>' for each candidate,
read the zone:* labels, and refuse to dispatch two agents whose zone labels intersect.

Per-task hint: tasks include their own 'PARALLEL' note describing what they can /
cannot run alongside. The orchestrator should read those notes before fanning out.

description:

Validates Σ(splits) == parent amount (in milliunits). Constructs ExistingTransaction with subtransactions and PATCHes.

Enables 'this Costco run was $120 groceries + $30 gas' decomposition.
